arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-objdump objdump 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The tombstone has frames from several .NET libraries:

1. `libmonosgen-2.0.so` - BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
2. `libSystem.Native.so` - BuildId: `7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213`
3. `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` - BuildId: `2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e`


I'll need to fetch the debug symbols for each library using their Build IDs from Microsoft's symbol server, then use `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to map the addresses to actual function names. The symbol server supports both a direct URL format and the debuginfod protocol for retrieving ELF symbols by build ID. I've got the build IDs for the other libraries now. Let me construct the proper download URLs using Microsoft's symbol server format and attempt to retrieve the symbol files, then figure out which symbolication tool will work best for parsing the stack trace.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-objdump objdump 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
Good, I have `llvm-symbolizer` and `addr2line`. Now I need to download the symbol files from Microsoft's symbol server.

Let me try to download the debug symbols for each library. The Microsoft symbol server URL format for ELF files is:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>`

Let me try downloading them. I'll start with all three libraries in parallel.

The libraries and their build IDs:
1. libmonosgen-2.0.so: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be


2. libSystem.Native.so: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213
3. libSystem.Globalization.Native.so: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e

Now I'll attempt to fetch the symbol files for all three libraries from the Microsoft symbol server.

arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c13edec89367440bb03386d8c686ffbd && mkdir -p symbols && \\\ncurl -sSL -o symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so\" -w \"\\nHTTP: %{http_code}\\nSize: %{size_download}\\n\" && \\\ncurl -sSL -o symbols/libSystem.Native.so \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Native.so/elf-buildid-7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213/libSystem.Native.so\" -w \"\\nHTTP: %{http_code}\\nSize: %{size_download}\\n\" && \\\ncurl -sSL -o symbols/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so/elf-buildid-2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so\" -w \"\\nHTTP: %{http_code}\\nSize: %{size_download}\\n\"", "description": "Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}

data.reasoningText:
The libmonosgen-2.0.so was downloaded successfully (3.1MB), but the other two returned 404. Let me check if the downloaded file is actually a valid ELF file and has debug symbols. Also, let me try alternative URL patterns for the other two.

Let me also try the _.debug format which is used for separate debug info files.

data.reasoningText:
I was able to symbolicate the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames. The other two libraries (`libSystem.Native.so` and `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so`) aren't available on the Microsoft symbol server - their build IDs might be from a different build or those specific symbols may not be published. However, let me try to use the dynamic symbols from the main binary for those. Let me also try downloading those files directly without the debug info variant.

Let me also try using the dynamic symbol table of the stripped monosgen binary for the libSystem.Native.so frames, but that won't work since they're different libraries.


I'm realizing the build IDs for System.Native and System.Globalization.Native look unusual - they might be synthetic or from test builds - which could explain why I can't find them on the symbol server. I couldn't retrieve the System.Native libraries due to 404 errors, so I'll focus on presenting the monosgen frames I successfully symbolicated along with the libc abort call that was already in the tombstone.

Looking at frame #03 more carefully, I see that llvm-symbolizer is returning multiple inlined functions on separate lines, so I need to consolidate those into a single frame entry showing the call chain through the signal handler and profiler code.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "# Clean up downloaded symbols\nrm -rf /tmp/sv-c13edec89367440bb03386d8c686ffbd/symbols", "description": "Clean up temp symbol files", "initial_wait": 5}

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace. I resolved the 4 **libmonosgen-2.0.so** frames using debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server:

```
#00 pc 0x06ae1c  libc.so                          abort+164
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so               ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x222234  libmonosgen-2.0.so               ves_icall_System_ArgIterator_Setup (icall.c:6353)
#03 pc 0x19bac0  libmonosgen-2.0.so               profiler_signal_handler (mini-posix.c:309)
                                                    ├─ mono_chain_signal (mini-posix.c:197) [inlined]
                                                    └─ get_saved_signal_handler (mini-posix.c:140) [inlined]
#04 pc 0x008f14  libSystem.Native.so              (symbols not available)
#05 pc 0x003b20  libSystem.Globalization.Native.so (symbols not available)
#06 pc 0x19bc14  libmonosgen-2.0.so               sampling_thread_func (mini-posix.c:640)
#07 pc 0x012a48  libSystem.Native.so              (symbols not available)
```

**Summary:** The crash originated in the Mono profiler's signal handler (`profiler_signal_handler`), which chained through `mono_chain_signal` and triggered `Environment.FailFast`, ultimately calling `abort()`. This pattern is typical of a fatal signal (SIGSEGV at address `0x0`) caught during profiler sampling.

**Note:** Symbols for `libSystem.Native.so` and `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` were not found on the Microsoft symbol server — their build IDs may be from a custom or unpublished build.
